notes: Batch 36 — MPM ARM ADJUDICATION BATCH. B35 produced the project's first usable MPM data and a DECISIVE STRUCTURAL NEGATIVE: the bare {secrete, sense, inflow_mpm, mpm} stack is NON-AGGREGATING at every tested parameter (Est #130; inner_mass FLAT 0.19, morphology IDENTICAL sparse-scatter across n_frames in [120, 2400]). The PROJECT-BEST LOSS 1.141 (sw 11 substeps=32) and the loss=1.17 at sw 15 youngs=5 (n_frames=1200) are both SUSPECTED particle-depletion artefacts (Est #140/#144 provisional). B36 ADJUDICATES THESE WINS and surveys joint parameter corners to rule out a narrow productive regime before committing to the B37 cohesion-mechanism edit (deferred to keep B36 infra surface stable). NO ENGINE CODE EDITS THIS BATCH. PRONGS: (alpha) ADJUDICATION sweeps 0-4 — does substeps=32 win survive n_frames=1200? does youngs=5 win survive across finer grid? are the wins 16-seed-robust? does the corner (substeps=32, youngs=5) compound? (beta) JOINT SWEEPS 5-7, 12-13 — survey sense.gain x {camp.D, secrete.rate, mpm.drag, mpm.a_max} at low-D corner for narrow productive niches; cell.n x n_frames=1200 stress test. (gamma) NEW PARENT VALIDATION 8-10, 14-15 — fine sweeps of camp.{D, decay}, inflow_mpm.rate, particle.per_parent at the NEW parent to confirm working values; the LIVE resolution knob `mpm.dt_sub` (sw 6, untested for 35 batches because dt was the bypassed surrogate). NEW PARENT (B36): per_parent=24, mpm.{a_max=1800, vmax=1.5, drag=10}, inflow=0.5, secrete=6, gain=50 — resolved working values from B35 metric ripples; cell.youngs=60 and mpm.substeps=8 UNCHANGED pending adjudication (not adopting suspected artefact wins).

sweeps.hypothesis: H1-B36 CENTRAL ADJUDICATION — does the sw 11 substeps=32 PROJECT-BEST LOSS 1.141 SURVIVE the n_frames=1200 stress test, or does the field empty entirely (particle-depletion artefact)? If loss at (substeps=32, n_frames=1200) is still ~1.14 and morphology shows MORE structure than at n_frames=240, Est #140 SUPPORTED. If loss explodes / n_mounds collapses to 1-2 / morphology completely empty, Est #140 RETRACTED as artefact and project-best loss reverts to 0.9126 (point-cell floor).

H2-B36 CENTRAL ADJUDICATION — does the sw 15 youngs=5 loss=1.17 win SURVIVE a fine-grained scan around 5 and at n_frames=1200? Refined around the apparent corner. If loss stays low only at one isolated point and morphology is sparse-scatter, particle-depletion artefact confirmed (Est #144 RETRACTED).

H3-B36 FINE — zooms substeps in [16, 64] at n_frames=240 (the B35 base evaluation point) to pin down the loss-corner shape. If the loss-vs-substeps curve has a SHARP isolated point at 32, with sparse morphology, it is a numerical instability point (artefact); if it shows a SMOOTH bowl with morphology improving with substeps, it is a genuine resolution effect.

H4-B36 — 16-seed verification at the project-best corner (mpm.substeps=32). If sigma_loss > 1.5 (the MPM seed-noise floor Est #143) the sw 11 win is within noise; if the win replicates across 16 seeds with low sigma it is a genuine corner. Adopts mpm.substeps=32 in the fixed overrides.

H5-B36 — 16-seed verification at the suspected JOINT corner (mpm.substeps=32, cell.youngs=5, n_frames=1200). If the two suspected artefact wins COMPOUND (loss << 1.14), they are likely the SAME particle-depletion mechanism (additive depletion). If they are INDEPENDENT mechanisms (loss ~ 1.14, mid between sw 11/sw 15), each may represent a separate physical effect; warrants further inspection.

H6-B36 — the LIVE MPM resolution knob, never swept in 35 batches because the engine outer `dt` was the (failed) surrogate (Est #139). Productive band identification. Default 2e-4; product substeps * dt_sub gates the cell-level integration accuracy.

H7-B36 — n_frames break test at the NEW B36 parent (with resolved working values per_parent=24, a_max=1800, vmax=1.5, drag=10, inflow=0.5, secrete=6, gain=50). The B35 sw 0 was at the OLD parent values; this re-checks whether ANY of the parent shifts moved the n_frames response curve into a productive regime (it should not, per Est #130 universal non-aggregation, but verify).

H8-B36 JOINT — sense.gain at low-D corner (camp.diffusion=0.001 — sw 3 best). The B35 sw 1 was at D=0.02 (base); this tests whether the steeper-gradient low-D regime opens a productive sense.gain band. If still flat morphology, Est #131 universally confirmed under the low-D corner.

H9-B36 JOINT — sense.gain at strong source (secrete.rate=20). If gain x secrete jointly opens a productive corner where neither alone did, the chemotactic SOURCE is the limiting factor; if still flat, Est #130 universally confirmed.
H10-B36 FINE — camp.diffusion fine sweep around 0.001 (sw 3 best) at NEW parent. Refines the working D band. If still flat morphology, the low-D corner is metric ripple only.
H11-B36 FINE — camp.decay fine sweep around 1.0 (sw 4 best) at NEW parent. Identifies whether decay has a productive corner at low D.
H12-B36 STRESS TEST — mpm.drag x n_frames=1200. Does longer integration with productive drag=10 give morphology? Est #135 predicts no.
H13-B36 JOINT — mpm.a_max at low-D corner (D=0.001). The B35 sw 12 found a_max=1800 best at base D; tests whether the steeper low-D gradient changes the productive band.
H14-B36 STRESS — cell.n x n_frames=1200 capped to ~1600 (Est #137 ceiling). Does longer integration + more cells produce visible aggregation? Per Est #130, no.
H15-B36 FINE — inflow_mpm.rate fine sweep around 0.5 (sw 5 best). Includes rate=0 ablation (no inflow, frame-0 cells only).

H16-B36 FINE — particle.per_parent fine sweep around the resolved working value 24 (sw 9 plateau). Confirms per_parent=24 is at the metric plateau and that the morphology is unchanged across all values (no morphology corner).
